text:
There was once a gentle old owl named Oddsworth. He lived deep in an oak forest renowned for its quiet. Few ventured nearer. One sunny morning, a young squirrel named Patch heard tales of Oddsworth's wisdom.

Patch came across an old gnome. Why do trees stay still? the gnome asked.

Because they know rain.

Yeah? Why?

Because they listen.

Oh. I've watched streams.

What do you know?

Nothing useful.

Then listen. I grow old. Spring arrives early. I sit by waters clear.

You're wise.

I've learned many things.

Like nothing matters?

Nothing but peace.

Then here's mine. Whenever sorrow comes, think of rain. It fades.

You’re silly.

But true.

There’s nothing greater.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
